Jordan has maintained a neutral stance in the Gaza conflict. While opposing Israeli aggression and sending humanitarian aid to Gaza, it also intercepted Iranian missiles to protect its own sovereignty—not to defend Israel. Jordan neither supports Hamas nor Iran militarily and aims to stay out of regional escalation.

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1283542228|1283542228]] by [[Special:Contributions/DecafPotato|DecafPotato]] ([[User talk:DecafPotato|talk]]) - the casualties section isn't broken down by 'side', it's by geographical region

make casualties in infobox only a total instead of by country/"side", because doing so is misleading without being able to explain it with text (e.g., there's no 'Gaza' entity in the belligerents, only Hamas, which governs and fights, so putting the Gaza Strip casualties on that 'side' of the infobox falsely implies that those killed in Gaza are affiliated with Hamas rather than say, the PA, which is on another 'side') — same issue exists for two Syrias, Hezbollah/Lebanon, etc.
